title: Fleet lifecycle event / agent status snapshot
description: The JSON an agent's lifecycle hooks emit on SessionStart / PreToolUse / PostToolUse / Stop, which the tasks cockpit consumes. Today this is materialized as a per-agent snapshot file (data/fleet/<handle>.json) OVERWRITTEN on every event — the snapshot IS the latest event. This schema models the event; a snapshot file is simply the most recent instance. 'offline' is NEVER written by a producer: the consumer derives it from updated_at staleness (>90s today). Producers MUST write the canonical host form (see host) and a lowercase handle — normalization must stop living in the consumer.

schema_version.description: Contract version. The live hook files (v0) omit it; consumers MUST treat absence as v0 and tolerate missing `event`/`task_id`.

handle.description: Canonical lowercase agent handle (e.g. 'janet'). For snapshot files the filename (<handle>.json) is authoritative and MUST match.

host.description: Machine label in CANONICAL form: '.N' for lab dot-hosts (never 'dotN' — that drift forced consumer-side normalization), otherwise a plain hostname (e.g. 'scout-pc', 'windows').

event.description: Which lifecycle hook produced this event. New in v1 (v0 files omit it); lets consumers distinguish 'agent finished a tool' from 'agent stopped'.

status.description: Producer-reported status. 'offline' is derived by the consumer from staleness and MUST NOT be written.

current_tool.description: Tool currently executing (e.g. 'Bash'); null unless status=working.

task_id.description: Tracker task the agent is currently working (spawn-from-task tie; LOCKED: every agent action ties to a task id). null when the agent holds no claimed task. New in v1. The cockpit may still join focus via activeLeases(); this field makes the tie explicit at the source.

session_id.description: The agent's Claude Code session id, when known.

started_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when this agent session started.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC — when this event was written. The consumer's staleness/offline derivation keys on this field.

$comment: additionalProperties true on purpose: many producers (hooks on different machines/versions) feed one consumer; unknown extra fields must be skipped, not rejected. Contrast manager-config, which is deny-unknown.
